items.title: Tensions mount at DRC frontline as ex-President Kabila meets M23 rebels | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>In an extended version of our report, we look at how the former president of the Democratic Republic of Congo, Joseph Kabila has returned to the country after two-years in self imposed exile, and why Kabila's homecoming is not being welcomed by everyone. 

The former president is accused of supporting the M23 rebels that have violently seized territory in the mineral-rich eastern part of the country. The human rights organisation Amnesty International accuses those rebels of killing, torturing, and forcibly displacing civilian detainees in the areas they now control.

items.title: More than 150 killed in flooding in Nigeria | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>More than 150 people have been killed in Nigeria, after torrential rain submerged Mokwa, a market town in the north.  

Residents were trying to clear away muddy debris after the rains subsided. Over three thousand people were forced to leave their homes when the flood waters rose to roof height following rains which started on Thursday. The floodwaters washed away roads and bridges. Nigeria's President Bola Tinubu said the army will help with the emergency response. 

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Hamas responds to US ceasefire proposal in Gaza | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Hamas has responded to a ceasefire proposal for Gaza -- put forward by US Middle East Envoy Steve Witkoff, and backed by Israel. 

Meanwhile, the World Food Program says 77 trucks loaded with flour were looted by hungry people in Gaza: "After nearly 80 days of a total blockade, communities are starving â€” and they are no longer willing to watch food pass them by." This marks the latest incident involving aid deliveries since Israel eased its 11-week blockade on the territory.

items.title: Death toll expected to rise after flash floods rip through Nigerian market town | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>More than a hundred people have been killed in Nigeria, after torrential rain submerged a market town in the north of the country. The military has been called in to help respond to what officials are calling 'unprecedented' flooding. Nigeria and West Africa in general are accustomed to seasonal downpours, but scientists say climate change is fueling more extreme weather.

00:00 Floods leave 100 dead with death toll expected to rise
02:17 DW's Olisa Chukwumah reports from Lagos

items.title: Ex-President Kabila returns to DRC amid accusations of supporting M23 | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The former president of the Democratic Republic of Congo, Joseph Kabila has returned to the country after two-years in self imposed exile. But Kabila's homecoming is not being welcomed by everyone. 
The former president is accused of supporting the M23 rebels that have violently seized territory in the mineral-rich eastern part of the country. The human rights organization Amnesty International accuses those rebels of killing, torturing, and forcibly displacing civilian detainees in the areas they now control.
Those areas include the big cities Goma in North Kivu and Bukavu in South Kivu. Neighboring countries are also involved. Rwanda denies it, but it is accused of backing the M23 rebel group, while Uganda has been helping the Congolese army fight the rebels. 
The political fallout from the conflict in the east has escalated with the Congolese government stripping former president Joseph Kabila of immunity. The government accuses Kabila of being linked to the M23 rebels. The Congolese senate voted to remove the immunity Kabila is entitled to as a former statesman. The move could lead to him being tried for treason and war crimes. Kabila denies being linked to M23 and accuses the government of President Felix Tshisekedi of stiffling democracy in the country.
DWâ€™s Jonas Gerding gained rare access, embedding with the Congolese military. He reports from Lubero, close to the frontline.

00:00 On the front in DRC
05:45 DW's Christine Mhundwa speaks with Kambale Musavuli, DRC native, human rights activist, and analyst with the Center for Research on Congo-Kinshasa based in Accra, Ghana

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: River trapped behind glacial debris in Swiss Alps threatens flooding | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Residents in the Swiss Alps appear to have been spared further catastrophe as water trapped behind glacial debris, which was threatening to flood a valley below, appears to be trickling away slowly.
Earlier this week, millions of cubic meters of ice, mud and rock flattened the village of Blatten. It had already been evacuated, but one person remains missing. Officials warned that fresh evacuations may be necessary as the mass was blocking a river and authorities feared it could trigger a flood or mudslides.

00:00 Glacial debris traps water in Swiss Alps
00:37 DW speaks with Christoph Hegg, environmental expert at the Swiss Federal Institute for Forest, Snow and Landscape Research

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Poland's presidential candidates hold their final campaign rallies | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Voters in Poland go to the polls on Sunday to elect a new president. Rafal Trzaskowski, Warsaw's centrist mayor and the government's prefered candidate, goes head-to-head with nationalist historian Karol Nawrocki, who's supported by the right-wing Law and Justice party. Friday was the final day of campaigning. The latest opinion polls show a razor-thin margin between the candidates. 

00:00 Presidential runoff in Poland

items.title: What has Elon Muskâ€™s DOGE actually achieved? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Tech billionaire Elon Musk is leaving his job as the US government's adivsor on efficienc, four months after promising to take a chainsaw to bureaucracy. Donald Trump praised Musk for his cost-cutting efforts during a farewell briefing in the Oval Office. During his time in Washington, Musk's Department of Government Efficiency - known as DOGE - fired thousands of government workers, prompting protests across America. 

00:00 Trump gives Musk Oval Office sendoff
00:59 Janelle Dumalaon, DW Correspondent
